---
title: "Quarto for Slides"
subtitle: "Reveal.js decks with executable code"
author: "Blood Doctor"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
    transition: fade
    code-line-numbers: false
    logo: ""
    footer: "Blood Doctor · Quarto"
---

## Why Quarto slides?

- Author in Markdown, present in the browser
- **Executable** code produces figures live at render
- Export to PDF with one flag

## Fragments reveal one by one

- First this point…
- …then this one…
- …and finally this.

## Live figure from Python {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
import numpy as np, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
x = np.linspace(0, 24, 200)
plt.figure(figsize=(6,3))
plt.plot(x, 100*np.exp(-np.log(2)/6*x), color="#B71C1C", lw=2)
plt.xlabel("Hours"); plt.ylabel("Activity (%)"); plt.title("Factor decay (t½ = 6 h)")
plt.grid(alpha=.3); plt.show()
```

## Two-column layout

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Intrinsic
- FXII, FXI, FIX, FVIII
- Measured by **APTT**
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Extrinsic
- Tissue factor, FVII
- Measured by **PT**
:::
::::

## Speaker notes & thanks

Press **S** for the speaker view.

::: {.notes}
Remind the audience that emicizumab invalidates one-stage FVIII assays.
:::
